No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to health outcomes or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a Master's level program, directly contributing to tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equipping students with advanced technical skills in information security for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to energy access or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Cryptographic protocols are foundational for technological upgrading (Target 8.2) and enable secure digital transactions, crucial for expanding access to financial services (Target 8.10) and fostering economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly focuses on designing and analyzing cryptographic protocols, essential for building resilient digital infrastructure (Target 9.1) and enhancing scientific research and technological capabilities in information security (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Cryptography, by enabling secure data storage and transmission, is an underlying technology for resilient digital infrastructure in smart cities, contributing indirectly to building resilient communities (Target 11.b).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to responsible consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to marine ecosystems or life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links cryptographic protocol design and analysis to terrestrial ecosystems or life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Cryptography is fundamental for protecting fundamental freedoms (Target 16.10), ensuring secure digital identity (Target 16.9), and building resilient, transparent institutions by combating cybercrime and unauthorized access (Targets 16.4, 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds advanced capacity in information and communications technology (Target 17.8) and facilitates knowledge sharing on a critical enabling technology (Target 17.6), supporting global partnerships for sustainable development.
